content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, there are NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) connected by N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 bidirectional roads in such a way that it is possible to reach any city from any other city. Each city has its own traditional dish for which it is famous; let's denote the tastiness of the dish in city iii by AiA_iAi‚Äč.

Chef is currently learning how to prepare tastier dishes. At any point, he can only remember how to cook one dish. Initially, the tastiness of the dish he can cook is WWW. Chef is going to travel from city 111 to city vvv. In each city he visits (including 111 and vvv), if the tastiness of the traditional dish of this city is strictly greater than the tastiness of the dish he can currently cook, then he forgets the dish he can currently cook and learns the traditional dish of this city.

Now, Chef is wondering how many times he is going to learn a new dish during his trip. You should answer QQQ independent queries. In each query, you are given the initial tastiness WWW and Chef's final destination vvv; compute the number of times Chef learns a new dish. Note that the input is given in a special format, so you have to answer the queries online.
constraints: 1‚Č§Pi‚Č§i‚ąí11 \le P_i \le i-11‚Č§Pi‚Äč‚Č§i‚ąí1 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of QQQ for all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 space-separated integers P2,P3,‚Ä¶,PNP_2, P_3, \dots, P_NP2‚Äč,P3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč, meaning there's an edge between iii and PiP_iPi‚Äč.
The fourth line contains one integer QQQ.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers AAA and BBB describing a query in the following format:

Let's denote the answer to the previous query by PrPrPr (Pr=0Pr=0Pr=0 if this is the first query).
For this query, v=A‚äēPrv = A \oplus Prv=A‚äēPr and W=B‚äēPrW = B \oplus PrW=B‚äēPr.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the number of times Chef learns a new dish.
